mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce corporate discount rates and lower borrowing costs, directly increasing the present value of future equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, aggregate consumer spending contracts sharply, reducing corporate earnings growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and rising consumer prices indicates robust demand outpacing supply constraints, allowing firms to pass costs to consumers and expand profit margins, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant at p > 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply chain throughput and capacity utilization, which reduces input cost pressures and boosts short-term equity valuations during periods of monetary accommodation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during easing periods, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with rising housing starts reflects broad-based economic expansion and increased capital expenditure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200 thousand indicate rapid labor market tightening and rising household income, which immediately boosts consumer sentiment and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS surges above 200 thousand, or the signal hit rate falls below 50 percent over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 265 while the federal funds rate remains near zero, markets price in imminent aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal deteriorating manufacturing output and weakening business investment, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested windows.
